meaning: vt.使成杂色,使有斑点n.杂色,斑点
examples: Multicolored scales mottle the bark of a sycamore tree near Lake Como, Italy.
Mottle are caused by poor ink or uneven absorption characteristics in the paper.
It was not dull grey as distance had suggested, but a mottle of khaki and black and olive-green.
Growing underground, it is prone to infestation by the feathery mottle virus and worms.
Into the sweet potato she spliced a gene from the pyrethrum, a white flower whose ingredients are fatal to insects and the feathery mottle virus.
